Meta.Documentation: A service test is an entity that exists for a controlled test invocation on a service. The service test is executed according to a schedule and contains service test configuration parameters that are to be applied at execution time, and service test measures that result. Skipped properties: id,href
The end date and time of the service test format: date-time
An indication of whether the service test is running in \"PROACTIVE\" or \"ONDEMAND\" mode
The name of the service test required
The start date and time of the service test. format: date-time
The actual state the service test is in. Recommended states are found in ExecutionStateType schema possible values include acknowledged, rejected, pending, inProgress, cancelled, completed and failed
List of characteristics with values that define the test run
The results of the test in terms of the measured metrics
When sub-classing, this defines the super-class
A URI to a JSON-Schema file that defines additional attributes and relationships format: uri
When sub-classing, this defines the sub-class Extensible name
